lines.speaker: t1_inspector_vos
lines.text: Your transponder's clean. I know. This is theatre.

lines.speaker: t1_inspector_vos
lines.text: Someone filed a priority override on your route. Means a Trust audit — but the override code traces to a genship signature, not a Trust one.

lines.speaker: t1_inspector_vos
lines.text: I'm supposed to hold you here while they verify. I'm not going to do that.

choices.label: "I have priority clearance. Check the file."
Stay quiet. See what else they offer.

lines.speaker: t1_inspector_vos
lines.text: Because the audit isn't about you. It's about whoever filed that override. They used a code that traces to a genship quartermaster — a supply officer who's been dead for six years.

lines.speaker: t1_inspector_vos
lines.text: Someone is using dead credentials to move ships through the belt. I want to know who. Letting you through is the fastest way to find out.

choices.label: "I'll keep an eye out. Send word if I find something."
"Not my problem. I'm just passing through."

lines.speaker: t1_inspector_vos
lines.text: NAC code. I should have guessed. Your people don't file overrides — they file requisitions and expect the universe to approve.

lines.speaker: t1_inspector_vos
lines.text: Pass through, Captain. And tell your quartermaster their dead friend's credentials are being used. They'll want to know.

choices.label: "I don't have a quartermaster. I'm not fleet."

lines.speaker: t1_inspector_vos
lines.text: ...You don't talk much. That's fine. Smart captains listen more than they speak.

lines.speaker: t1_inspector_vos
lines.text: Here's a tip: if someone's tracking your route, it's not T1. We don't have the budget for deep-belt surveillance. Check which Trust corp has a station on your vector.

lines.speaker: t1_inspector_vos
lines.text: Good hunting, Captain. The belt doesn't care who you were before. It only cares what you do next.
